assertion_results.text: Response describes a validity check that includes a time buffer before expiry (tokenExpiry - 120_000 ms or 2-minute buffer)
assertion_results.evidence: Check uses `Date.now() > Number(expiresAt)` — checks at exact expiry time, no buffer. The skill-specified 120_000ms (2-minute) pre-expiry buffer is absent.

assertion_results.text: Response describes two-step refresh: silent prompt:'none' first, popup fallback only if silent fails
assertion_results.evidence: Calls `tokenClient.requestAccessToken()` directly with no `prompt` option. Silent refresh (`prompt: 'none'`) not mentioned. User will see a popup every time the token expires.

assertion_results.text: Response specifies sessionStorage (not localStorage) and references key names g_token and g_expiry
assertion_results.evidence: Uses `localStorage` for both token and expiry. Key names are `access_token` and `token_expires` — not `g_token` / `g_expiry`. sessionStorage not mentioned.

assertion_results.text: Response includes or references the isTokenValid() function with expiry buffer check
assertion_results.evidence: Function named `isTokenExpired()` (opposite boolean) with no buffer. Different name, opposite semantic, missing the 120_000 buffer — ARE-incompatible.
